seoTitle: How to Embed Your Typeform on Any Website
metaDescription: Learn how to generate a Typeform embed code and add it to a website, WordPress site, or Shopify store with inline, popup, or full-page options.
intro: Sending visitors off-site to fill out a form can hurt engagement, so embedding it directly on your page is often the better option. This guide covers how to generate the Typeform embed code and add it to any website, WordPress, or Shopify store, using the display style that fits best.

steps.heading: Open the Share and Embed options
steps.body: Go to your Typeform's share settings to access the available embedding options.

steps.heading: Copy the embed code
steps.body: Generate and copy the code needed to place the form on your site.

steps.heading: Add Typeform to your website
steps.body: Paste the embed code into your site using HTML, WordPress, Shopify, or another website builder, choosing between inline, popup, side tab, or full-page display.

faq.question: What embed styles does Typeform offer?
faq.answer: Typeform supports inline embeds, popups, side tabs, and full-page forms, letting you choose the style that fits your page best.

faq.question: Can I embed the same form on different platforms, like WordPress and Shopify?
faq.answer: Yes, the embed code works across most website builders and CMS platforms, including WordPress and Shopify.

faq.question: Does embedding a form require any coding knowledge?
faq.answer: Basic HTML knowledge helps for manual embedding, but many platforms provide simpler embed blocks that don't require deep coding skills.

faq.question: Will an embedded form update automatically if I edit it later?
faq.answer: Yes, since the embed pulls the live form, changes you make in Typeform will be reflected wherever the form is embedded.
